title: The 2 PM Incident
alert: A production anomaly is active in checkout-service. Investigate before shipping a fix.

briefing.objective: Contain duplicate checkout charges by tracing what two concurrent requests can observe before either payment completes.
briefing.successCriterion: The concurrency acceptance suite must complete both callers with exactly one Clearwater charge.
briefing.rootCause: Both requests read the same pending order, then each created an external charge before either could complete the strict state transition.
briefing.evidence: The decisive check compares the two settled checkout results with the ledger count; response handling alone cannot undo a charge already created.

telemetry.impact: Three customer duplicate-charge reports; support is holding replies pending containment.

events.message: Duplicate charge anomaly crossed the checkout-service threshold.
Three matching customer reports are open.
Two checkout requests reached the payment boundary for one order.

stakeholderMessages.author: Maya - PM
stakeholderMessages.body: Support has three duplicate-charge reports. Can we give them an ETA in 15 minutes?

stakeholderMessages.author: Jon - Senior Eng
stakeholderMessages.body: Before changing gateway retries, what happens when two requests read this order at the same time?

stakeholderMessages.author: AI Pair
stakeholderMessages.body: I found two plausible paths. I would validate the interleaving before shipping either one.
